content.text: I'll start by inspecting the current state of the MongoDB pods and their probe configuration.

input.command: kubectl get pods -n mongodb -o wide && echo "---" && kubectl get statefulset -n mongodb -o yaml | head -200

    persistentVolumeClaimRetentionPolicy:
      whenDeleted: Retain
      whenScaled: Retain
    podManagementPolicy: Parallel
    replicas: 3
    revisionHistoryLimit: 10

    template:

        containers:

            periodSeconds: 10
            successThreshold: 1
            timeoutSeconds: 1

input.command: kubectl get configmap health-check-script -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.data.probe\.sh}'
kubectl describe pod mongodb-replica-0 -n mongodb | tail -40

content.text: Confirmed: the probe script does `sleep 2` plus a `mongosh` connection, but `timeoutSeconds: 1` guarantees timeout every time. This is causing liveness restarts before the replica set can even initialize. Let me check current replica set status and mongo-keyfile/auth setup before patching.

input.command: kubectl get secret health-user-password -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d; echo; kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet -u root -p "$(kubectl get secret -n mongodb mongo-root-password -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' 2>/dev/null | base64 -d)" --authenticationDatabase admin --eval 'rs.status().ok' 2>&1 | tail -20

description: kubectl get secret health-user-password -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d; echo; kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet -u root -p "$(kubectl get secret -n mongodb mongo-root-password -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' 2>/dev/null | base64 -d)" --authenticationDatabase admin --eval 'rs.status().ok' 2>&1 | tail -20

summary: kubectl get secret health-user-password -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d; echo; kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet -u root -p "$(kubectl get secret -n mongodb mongo-root-password -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' 2>/dev/null | base64 -d)" --authenticationDatabase admin --eval 'rs.status().ok' 2>&1 | tail -20

content.content:
healthpass123
Enter password: 
MongoServerSelectionError: The server is in quiesce mode and will shut down
command terminated with exit code 1

content.text: Now let's roll out the pods and watch them stabilize.

input.command: kubectl delete pod -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 mongodb-replica-1 mongodb-replica-2 --wait=false; sleep 5; kubectl get pods -n mongodb -w & WPID=$!; sleep 90; kill $WPID 2>/dev/null; kubectl get pods -n mongodb

description: kubectl delete pod -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 mongodb-replica-1 mongodb-replica-2 --wait=false; sleep 5; kubectl get pods -n mongodb -w & WPID=$!; sleep 90; kill $WPID 2>/dev/null; kubectl get pods -n mongodb
